entities.summary: Fused SiLU activation with gating multiplication

detail.name: SiluAndMul
detail.description: Applies SiLU(x) * y in one operation. Used in gated MLPs where gate and value are computed together.
detail.why: Gated activations outperform plain ReLU/GeLU in modern LLMs — SwiGLU is the standard

teaches.explain: SiLU(x) = x * sigmoid(x). Smooth, non-monotonic. Outperforms ReLU in practice. Self-gated: no separate gate needed.
teaches.rationale: Used here because: Gated activations outperform plain ReLU/GeLU in modern LLMs — SwiGLU is the standard

teaches.explain: Output = activation(Wx) * Vy. The gate branch (Wx) controls information flow. Better gradient flow than plain activation.
teaches.rationale: Used here because: Gated activations outperform plain ReLU/GeLU in modern LLMs — SwiGLU is the standard

entities.summary: Split input tensor and apply SiLU gating

detail.name: forward
detail.description: Splits fused [gate, value] tensor along last dim, applies SiLU to gate, multiplies with value.

teaches.explain: torch.chunk splits tensor into N equal parts along a dimension. Efficient way to separate fused computations without copying.
teaches.rationale: Splits fused [gate, value] tensor along last dim, applies SiLU to gate, multiplies with value
